Title: Add a drug-class allergen (Penicillins) rather than a single drug and verify class-level coding
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient dashboard open; allergen concept set includes drug classes
Test_Steps: 1. Open Allergies widget | 2. Add New Allergy | 3. In allergen search type "Penicillins" | 4. Select the drug-class concept (not the individual product) | 5. Add reaction and severity | 6. Save
Expected_Result: Allergy persists referencing the drug-class concept; list shows class-level allergen so all member drugs are later cross-checked, not only one product

Title: Allergen coded concept maps to a non-retired RxNorm/SNOMED reference term
Preconditions: DB/concept access available; coded allergen recorded
Test_Steps: 1. Record a coded drug allergy | 2. Query concept_reference_map for the allergen concept | 3. Verify a mapped reference term in RxNorm or SNOMED CT | 4. Confirm source term is not retired
Expected_Result: Allergen concept carries a SAME-AS mapping to an active RxNorm/SNOMED term; no retired or missing mapping that would break cross-checks

Title: Reject saving a coded allergen whose underlying concept is retired
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; a retired allergen concept exists in the dictionary
Test_Steps: 1. Open Allergies widget | 2. Add New Allergy | 3. Attempt to select a retired allergen concept | 4. Attempt Save
Expected_Result: Retired concept is not offered in the picker, or save is blocked with a clear message; no allergy stored against a retired concept

Title: Distinguish per-reaction severity from overall criticality on a single allergen
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient dashboard open
Test_Steps: 1. Add allergy to Sulfa | 2. Add reaction Hives with severity Mild | 3. Add reaction Difficulty breathing with severity Severe | 4. Set overall criticality High | 5. Save | 6. Re-open the saved allergy
Expected_Result: Each reaction retains its own severity and the allergen retains a separate High criticality; severity and criticality are not conflated

Title: Decision table mapping severity and reaction type to overall criticality
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; criticality derivation rules configured
Test_Steps: 1. Create allergy: Mild + cutaneous reaction | 2. Create allergy: Moderate + GI reaction | 3. Create allergy: Severe + respiratory reaction | 4. Create allergy: any severity + Anaphylaxis | 5. Observe derived/required criticality for each
Expected_Result: Mild->Low criticality; Moderate->Low/Unable-to-assess; Severe->High; Anaphylaxis forces High criticality regardless of stated severity per rule table

Title: Boundary check on allergy onset date rejecting a future date
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient dashboard open; current date 2026-07-01
Test_Steps: 1. Add New Allergy | 2. Select allergen | 3. Enter onset date in the future | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Save is blocked or warns; onset date cannot be in the future relative to server date

Title: Onset date equal to date of birth boundary is accepted
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient DOB known
Test_Steps: 1. Add allergy | 2. Set onset date equal to patient DOB | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Allergy saves; congenital/birth onset boundary is permitted and stored exactly

Title: Toggle NKA off then re-assert NKA after voiding the only allergy
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient currently No Known Allergies
Test_Steps: 1. From NKA, add an allergy (clears NKA) | 2. Void that allergy with a reason | 3. Re-assert No Known Allergies | 4. Verify final state
Expected_Result: State transitions NKA -> Has-Allergy -> (void) -> NKA cleanly; final widget shows No Known Allergies with no residual active allergy

Title: Distinguish No Known Allergies from unassessed (no allergy data) status
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; one patient explicitly marked NKA and one patient never assessed
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient A marked NKA | 2. Note widget status | 3. Open patient B never assessed | 4. Note widget status | 5. Compare
Expected_Result: NKA shows an explicit asserted "No Known Allergies"; unassessed patient shows a distinct "not recorded/unknown" prompt, never silently implying no allergies

Title: API rejects asserting NKA when active allergies exist
Preconditions: Valid session; patient UUID with an existing active allergy
Test_Steps: 1. POST NKA assertion via REST for the patient | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: API returns 4xx with a conflict/validation error; NKA flag not set while active allergies remain

Title: Block exact-duplicate allergen even when case/spacing of free-text differs
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient already has Other free-text allergen "Bee venom"
Test_Steps: 1. Add New Allergy | 2. Select Other | 3. Enter "  bee VENOM " with different case and padding | 4. Save
Expected_Result: System detects the normalized duplicate and warns/blocks; no second near-identical free-text allergen is created

Title: Allow same allergen if a prior entry for it was voided
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient had Penicillin allergy that is now voided
Test_Steps: 1. Confirm prior Penicillin allergy is voided | 2. Add New Allergy for Penicillin | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Re-adding the previously voided allergen is permitted; not treated as an active duplicate

Title: Voiding an allergy requires a non-empty void reason
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has an active allergy
Test_Steps: 1. Open the allergy | 2. Click Remove/Delete | 3. Leave void reason blank | 4. Confirm
Expected_Result: Void is blocked until a reason is supplied; mandatory void-reason enforced and later auditable

Title: Voided allergy is excluded from active drug-allergy cross-check
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient's Penicillin allergy has been voided; ordering available
Test_Steps: 1. Confirm Penicillin allergy is voided | 2. Start a drug order for Amoxicillin | 3. Observe interaction checking
Expected_Result: No allergy alert fires from the voided entry; voided allergies do not participate in cross-check, preventing false alerts

Title: Edit history of an allergy preserves prior values for audit
Preconditions: DB/audit access; an allergy that has been edited at least once
Test_Steps: 1. Edit an allergy changing severity Mild->Severe | 2. Query allergy/audit tables | 3. Verify prior value retained or change recorded
Expected_Result: Change is auditable with old and new values, editing user and timestamp; prior clinical value is not silently lost

Title: Direct drug-allergy match triggers an alert at order entry
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has active Penicillin allergy; CDS/interaction checking enabled; active visit
Test_Steps: 1. Open Orders | 2. Add a drug order for Penicillin V | 3. Proceed toward signing
Expected_Result: An allergy interaction alert is presented before the order is signed, naming the matching allergen and reaction

Title: Class-level allergy triggers alert when ordering a class member drug
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has Penicillins (class) allergy; ordering enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Open Orders | 2. Order Amoxicillin (a penicillin-class member) | 3. Observe alert
Expected_Result: Cross-check resolves the ordered drug to its class and fires the allergy alert even though the exact product was not the recorded allergen

Title: Cross-reactivity alert for cephalosporin in a penicillin-allergic patient
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has Penicillin allergy; cross-reactivity rules configured
Test_Steps: 1. Open Orders | 2. Order Cefalexin (cephalosporin) | 3. Observe whether a cross-reactivity advisory appears
Expected_Result: A cross-reactivity advisory (potential beta-lactam cross-sensitivity) is surfaced; clinician can review before signing

Title: No alert when ordering an unrelated drug for an allergic patient
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has Penicillin allergy only
Test_Steps: 1. Open Orders | 2. Order Paracetamol (unrelated) | 3. Observe alerts
Expected_Result: No allergy alert fires; cross-check does not raise false positives for unrelated agents

Title: NKA patient ordering generates no allergy alert
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient explicitly marked No Known Allergies
Test_Steps: 1. Confirm NKA | 2. Order any drug | 3. Observe alerts
Expected_Result: No drug-allergy alert; NKA suppresses allergy alerting while still permitting other interaction checks

Title: Unassessed allergy status surfaces a prompt rather than silent no-alert
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient allergies never assessed
Test_Steps: 1. Confirm allergies not recorded | 2. Start a drug order | 3. Observe whether system prompts to record allergy status
Expected_Result: System prompts/flags that allergy status is unknown before signing rather than implying safety with silence

Title: Decision table for alert action by criticality and clinician choice
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; allergy alerting with override workflow enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Order drug matching a High-criticality allergy and choose Override | 2. Order drug matching a Low-criticality allergy and choose Override | 3. Order matching High-criticality and choose Cancel | 4. Record outcomes
Test_Data: Criticality x Action: High/Override; Low/Override; High/Cancel
Expected_Result: High-criticality override requires a documented reason (hard stop with reason); Low-criticality override may be soft; Cancel aborts the order in all cases per rule table

Title: Override of a high-criticality allergy alert mandates an override reason
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has High-criticality Penicillin allergy; order matching it in progress
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger the allergy alert | 2. Choose Override | 3. Leave override reason blank | 4. Attempt to proceed
Expected_Result: Proceeding is blocked until a free-text override reason is entered; empty override is rejected

Title: Override reason and overriding user are captured to the audit trail
Preconditions: DB/audit access; an override of an allergy alert has occurred
Test_Steps: 1. Override a high-criticality allergy alert with reason | 2. Query the audit/override store | 3. Verify captured fields
Expected_Result: Audit record captures patient, ordered drug, matched allergen, override reason, user and UTC timestamp

Title: Severe drug-drug interaction matrix triggers a hard interaction alert
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient already on Warfarin; DDI checking enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Order a drug with a known severe interaction (e.g. Aspirin) | 2. Observe interaction severity classification | 3. Repeat with a moderate-interaction drug | 4. Repeat with a no-interaction drug
Expected_Result: Severe pair yields a hard/critical alert; moderate yields an advisory; none yields no alert, per interaction matrix classification

Title: Interaction check considers active medication list, not just current order
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has an active Warfarin order on the medication list
Test_Steps: 1. Open Orders | 2. Add new Aspirin order | 3. Observe whether interaction references existing active Warfarin
Expected_Result: Interaction is detected against the existing active medication, confirming the check spans the whole active list

Title: Discontinued/expired medications are excluded from interaction checking
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient's Warfarin order is discontinued/expired
Test_Steps: 1. Confirm Warfarin is discontinued | 2. Order Aspirin | 3. Observe alerts
Expected_Result: No DDI alert from the inactive Warfarin; only active medications participate, preventing stale false positives

Title: Pairwise coverage of interaction severity x clinician action x route
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; DDI checking enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Construct order scenarios pairwise across interaction severity, clinician action and order route | 2. Execute each | 3. Verify alert and outcome
Expected_Result: Each pairwise combination behaves per rules with no crash, missing alert or inconsistent outcome across routes

Title: Drug-condition contraindication alert (NSAID with active peptic ulcer)
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient has active condition Peptic ulcer disease; CDS rules configured
Test_Steps: 1. Open Orders | 2. Order Ibuprofen | 3. Observe contraindication advisory referencing the condition
Expected_Result: A drug-condition contraindication advisory is surfaced citing the active condition before signing

Title: Pregnancy contraindication advisory for a category-X drug
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient flagged pregnant; teratogenicity rules configured
Test_Steps: 1. Confirm pregnancy status | 2. Order a known teratogenic drug (e.g. Isotretinoin) | 3. Observe advisory
Expected_Result: A pregnancy contraindication advisory is surfaced; clinician must acknowledge before proceeding

Title: Duplicate-therapy alert when ordering a second drug of the same ingredient
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient already on Paracetamol; duplicate therapy checking enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Order a combination product also containing Paracetamol | 2. Observe duplicate therapy advisory
Expected_Result: A duplicate/therapeutic-overlap advisory is surfaced warning of cumulative dosing of the shared ingredient

Title: Interaction check completes within acceptable latency at order entry
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient with 10 active meds and 5 allergies; ordering enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Add a new drug order | 2. Measure time from add to alert evaluation completion | 3. Repeat 10 times | 4. Compute p95
Expected_Result: Interaction evaluation completes within the agreed SLA (e.g. <=2s p95) and does not block the UI; no missed alerts under load

Title: Reconcile imported external allergies against existing local allergies
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; external allergy list received for an established patient
Test_Steps: 1. Open allergy reconciliation | 2. Review incoming vs existing allergies | 3. Mark a match to keep | 4. Add a new one | 5. Mark a stale one for void | 6. Confirm
Expected_Result: Reconciliation merges without duplicating matched allergens, adds the new one, and voids/flags the stale one with audit of the reconciliation action

Title: Reconciliation conflict where severity differs between sources
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; incoming source lists same allergen with different severity
Test_Steps: 1. Start reconciliation | 2. Observe conflict on Penicillin severity Mild(local) vs Severe(incoming) | 3. Apply resolution rule | 4. Confirm
Expected_Result: Conflict is surfaced; resolution rule (e.g. take most-severe/most-recent) is applied transparently and the chosen value is recorded with provenance

Title: FHIR AllergyIntolerance clinicalStatus and verificationStatus are populated
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint enabled; patient has an active confirmed allergy
Test_Steps: 1. GET /ws/fhir2/R4/AllergyIntolerance?patient={id} | 2. Inspect clinicalStatus and verificationStatus | 3. Validate codes against the FHIR value sets
Expected_Result: Resource carries clinicalStatus=active and verificationStatus=confirmed using the canonical FHIR code systems; values are not null

Title: Voided allergy maps to FHIR verificationStatus entered-in-error
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint enabled; an allergy voided as entered-in-error exists
Test_Steps: 1. Void an allergy with reason Entered in error | 2. GET the AllergyIntolerance resource | 3. Inspect verificationStatus
Expected_Result: Resource verificationStatus maps to entered-in-error (or the entry is excluded per policy); voided clinical state is represented correctly to consumers

Title: FHIR AllergyIntolerance type and category fields are correct
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint enabled; drug and food allergies recorded
Test_Steps: 1. GET AllergyIntolerance for a drug allergy and a food allergy | 2. Inspect type and category | 3. Validate codes
Expected_Result: Drug allergy has type=allergy and category=medication; food allergy has category=food; codes drawn from the correct FHIR value sets

Title: Create AllergyIntolerance via FHIR POST persists and is retrievable
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint enabled; write privilege; patient reference valid
Test_Steps: 1. POST a valid AllergyIntolerance resource | 2. Capture the returned id | 3. GET the resource by id | 4. Verify fields round-trip
Expected_Result: POST returns 201 with a logical id; GET returns the same coded allergen, criticality and status; round-trip is lossless

Title: Reject FHIR AllergyIntolerance with an unbound criticality code
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint enabled; write privilege
Test_Steps: 1. POST AllergyIntolerance with criticality not in the value set | 2. Inspect response and OperationOutcome
Expected_Result: Server returns 4xx with an OperationOutcome describing the invalid code; no resource created

Title: Inbound HL7 v2 AL1 segment creates a patient allergy
Preconditions: HL7 v2 inbound interface configured; AL1-capable ADT message accepted
Test_Steps: 1. Send an ADT message with an AL1 allergy segment | 2. Process the message | 3. Open the patient allergies
Expected_Result: Allergy from AL1 is created with allergen, severity and reaction mapped; message is acknowledged with AA

Title: HL7 AL1 with unmapped severity code is rejected or defaulted safely
Preconditions: HL7 v2 inbound interface configured
Test_Steps: 1. Send ADT with AL1 severity code outside the configured table | 2. Process | 3. Inspect ACK and resulting allergy
Expected_Result: Message is rejected (AE/AR) or severity defaults to unknown with a flagged data-quality note; no silently wrong severity is stored

Title: Multiple AL1 segments in one message create multiple allergies
Preconditions: HL7 v2 inbound interface configured
Test_Steps: 1. Send a message containing two AL1 segments | 2. Process | 3. Verify both allergies created
Expected_Result: Both allergies are created with correct set IDs and no overwrite/loss of the second segment

Title: REST update of allergy severity reflects in subsequent GET
Preconditions: Valid session; patient UUID with an existing allergy UUID
Test_Steps: 1. POST/PUT update to the allergy changing severity | 2. GET the allergy | 3. Compare
Expected_Result: Update succeeds (200) and GET returns the new severity; no stale cached value returned

Title: Allergy API rejects mismatched allergen category and concept
Preconditions: Valid session; patient UUID known
Test_Steps: 1. POST allergy with category Food but a drug allergen concept | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: Server returns a validation error; inconsistent category/concept pairing is not persisted

Title: Allergy override audit is tamper-evident and immutable to clinicians
Preconditions: Two roles: Clinician and a user with audit-read only; an override record exists
Test_Steps: 1. As Clinician attempt to edit/delete the override audit record via UI/API | 2. Verify it cannot be altered | 3. As audit reader view the record
Expected_Result: Clinicians cannot modify or delete override audit entries; record is immutable and remains intact for compliance

Title: IDOR check: clinician cannot read another patient's allergy by UUID
Preconditions: Valid session for Clinician without access to target patient; another patient's allergy UUID known
Test_Steps: 1. GET the other patient's allergy by direct UUID via REST | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: Server returns 403/404; no PHI for the unauthorized patient is disclosed via direct object reference

Title: Free-text allergen comment is sanitized against stored XSS
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient dashboard open
Test_Steps: 1. Add allergy with Other free-text containing a script payload | 2. Save | 3. Re-open the allergy list and detail
Expected_Result: Payload is stored/encoded as inert text and never executes when the allergy list/detail renders

Title: Allergy interaction alert is announced to screen readers as an alert
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; screen reader active; patient with allergy; ordering a matching drug
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger an allergy alert during order entry | 2. Observe screen reader behavior | 3. Verify focus and role
Expected_Result: Alert uses an assertive live region/role=alert so it is announced immediately; focus moves to or references the alert; not silently visual-only

Title: Severity and criticality are not conveyed by color alone
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; allergy list with varying severities displayed
Test_Steps: 1. View allergy list with Mild/Moderate/Severe entries | 2. Inspect indicators | 3. Verify text/icon accompanies any color cue
Expected_Result: Each severity/criticality has a text label or icon in addition to color, meeting WCAG use-of-color; distinguishable without color perception

Title: Exploratory: concurrent allergy edits from two sessions on same patient
Preconditions: Two clinician sessions open on the same patient allergy
Test_Steps: 1. In session A edit allergy severity | 2. In session B edit the same allergy reaction before A saves | 3. Save both | 4. Inspect final state and any conflict handling
Expected_Result: No lost update silently overwrites the other change; system either merges, locks, or surfaces a stale-data conflict; final state is coherent and auditable

Title: Exploratory: ordering during alert evaluation race and double-submit
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient with matching allergy; ordering enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Add a matching drug order | 2. Rapidly double-click sign before the alert renders | 3. Observe whether order can bypass the alert
Expected_Result: Alert cannot be bypassed by a double-submit/race; order is not signed without the allergy check completing; no duplicate order created

Title: Pediatric weight-based order still cross-checks allergy independent of dose
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; pediatric patient with Penicillin allergy; weight recorded
Test_Steps: 1. Open Orders for the pediatric patient | 2. Enter a weight-based Amoxicillin order | 3. Observe allergy alert regardless of computed dose
Expected_Result: Allergy cross-check fires based on the allergen/class match irrespective of the weight-based dose calculation

Title: Unable-to-assess criticality is a valid distinct value
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; patient dashboard open
Test_Steps: 1. Add allergy | 2. Set criticality to Unable to assess | 3. Save | 4. Re-open
Expected_Result: Allergy stores criticality as unable-to-assess distinctly from low/high and from null; value persists and renders correctly

Title: Override does not propagate: a new order matching the same allergy re-alerts
Preconditions: Logged in as Clinician; clinician overrode an allergy alert for one order earlier
Test_Steps: 1. After a prior override, start a NEW order matching the same allergy | 2. Observe whether the alert re-fires
Test_Data: Allergy=Penicillin; prior override exists; NewOrder=Penicillin V
Expected_Result: The new order re-triggers the allergy alert; a prior override does not suppress future alerts for separate orders
